humanities_role_explanation: Evaluates multimodal models on the domain-specific task of interpreting and classifying Christian iconography (subject matter and symbolic meaning), treating historical religious artwork as visual content.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Multi-modal classification combining images of Christian religious paintings with text prompts containing class labels or detailed Iconclass descriptions.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Assesses fine-grained visual recognition of Christian saints by matching visual features in artwork to iconographic concepts. Tasks include zero-shot classification (using simple saint names or detailed descriptive texts) and few-shot in-context learning.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specifically designed to evaluate domain-specific performance on Christian iconography, not a general-purpose vision-language benchmark.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates OpenAI GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, Google Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Gemini 2.5 Flash.

llm_is_primary_system.note: Multimodal generative LLMs (GPT and Gemini models) and contrastive VLMs (CLIP, SigLIP) are the primary systems being benchmarked.

arts_humanities_data.note: Focuses on Visual Arts and Performing Arts and Religious Studies, evaluating Christian religious paintings annotated with Iconclass codes.

quantitative_metrics.note: Uses classification Accuracy and cross-dataset consistency (prediction stability on near-duplicate images).

published_after_march_2023.note: The preprint was published in September 2025.

english_language.note: The paper and prompt templates are written in English.

summary: This study benchmarks Vision-Language Models and Multimodal Large Language Models on single-label image classification of Christian iconography. Using subsets of ArtDL, ICONCLASS, and Wikidata, the authors evaluate models in zero-shot and few-shot settings to assess their potential for metadata curation in digital humanities. The findings show that advanced LLMs can outperform fine-tuned baselines, though performance is highly sensitive to dataset consistency and few-shot exemplar selection.
key_findings: Gemini 2.5 Pro and GPT-4o achieved peak accuracies of 90.45% and 88.20% on the ArtDL dataset, respectively, outperforming fine-tuned ResNet50 baselines. However, performance dropped significantly on the Wikidata dataset, where the contrastive model SigLIP-SO400M-Patch14-384 performed best (66.29%). Adding rich iconographic descriptions generally improved zero-shot accuracy, while few-shot learning unexpectedly degraded or only minimally improved performance for most LLMs.
review_notes: No significant disagreements. Merged the analytical tasks list for clarity, and included Cip's addition of 'Religious Studies' to the ASJC areas since the dataset explicitly curates Christian saints and religious themes.
